az.title: ∆Źn qńĪsa yol alqoritml…ôri
az.description: Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford v…ô dig…ôr …ôn qńĪsa yol alqoritml…ôri √ß…ôkili qraflarda …ôn qńĪsa yolu tapmaq √ľ√ß√ľn istifad…ô olunur, Dijkstra il…ô …ôn qńĪsa yol problemi il…ô izah edilir.

codes.php:
<?php

function dijkstra($graph, $start, $target) {
  $vertices = count($graph);
  $dist = array_fill(0, $vertices, PHP_INT_MAX);
  $visited = array_fill(0, $vertices, false);
  $dist[$start] = 0;
  for ($count = 0; $count < $vertices - 1; $count++) {
    $min = PHP_INT_MAX;
    $u = 0;
    for ($i = 0; $i < $vertices; $i++) {
      if (!$visited[$i] && $dist[$i] <= $min) {
        $min = $dist[$i];
        $u = $i;
      }
    }
    $visited[$u] = true;
    for ($v = 0; $v < $vertices; $v++) {
      if (!$visited[$v] && $graph[$u][$v] && $dist[$u] != PHP_INT_MAX && $dist[$u] + $graph[$u][$v] < $dist[$v]) {
        $dist[$v] = $dist[$u] + $graph[$u][$v];
      }
    }
  }
  if ($dist[$target] == PHP_INT_MAX) {
    echo "Target not reachable\n";
  } else {
    echo "Distance to target: " . $dist[$target] . "\n";
  }
}

$graph = [
  [0, 4, 2, 0, 0],
  [4, 0, 5, 10, 0],
  [2, 5, 0, 3, 8],
  [0, 10, 3, 0, 6],
  [0, 0, 8, 6, 0]
];
dijkstra($graph, 0, 4);
// Output: Distance to target: 8

content.text: ∆Źn qńĪsa yol alqoritml…ôri n…ôdir?

Qraf n√∂qt…ôl…ôr (vertices) v…ô onlarńĪn arasńĪndakńĪ √ß…ôkili k…ônarlardan (edges) ibar…ôtdir, m…ôs…ôl…ôn, Ňü…ôh…ôrl…ôri v…ô yollarńĪn m…ôsaf…ôl…ôrini t…ômsil ed…ô bil…ôr. ∆Źn m…ôŇühur alqoritml…ôr Dijkstra v…ô Bellman-Ford-dur. Dijkstra alqoritmi m√ľsb…ôt √ß…ôkili qraflarda …ôn qńĪsa yolu tapńĪr v…ô a√ßg√∂zl√ľ (greedy) strategiyadan istifad…ô edir. Bellman-Ford is…ô m…ônfi √ß…ôkil…ôri d…ô d…ôst…ôkl…ôyir v…ô m…ônfi d√∂vr√ľ aŇükarlaya bilir. M…ôs…ôl…ôn, Dijkstra il…ô bir Ňü…ôh…ôrd…ôn dig…ôrin…ô …ôn qńĪsa m…ôsaf…ô tapńĪla bil…ôr (m…ôs…ôl…ôn, 0-dan 4-…ô 8 vahid m…ôsaf…ô). Backtracking il…ô m√ľqayis…ôd…ô …ôn qńĪsa yol alqoritml…ôri daha s…ôm…ôr…ôlidir, √ß√ľnki b√ľt√ľn m√ľmk√ľn yollarńĪ yoxlamńĪr. Dinamik proqramlaŇüdńĪrma il…ô m√ľqayis…ôd…ô is…ô Dijkstra daha az yaddaŇü t…ôl…ôb edir, lakin Bellman-Ford daha √ßox hesablama t…ôl…ôb ed…ô bil…ôr.

M…ôs…ôl…ôn, 5 n√∂qt…ôli qrafda 0-dan 4-…ô yol 0 ‚Üí 2 ‚Üí 4 (m…ôsaf…ô 8) ola bil…ôr. Bellman-Ford is…ô b√ľt√ľn k…ônarlarńĪ d…ôf…ôl…ôrl…ô yoxlayńĪr v…ô m…ônfi √ß…ôkil…ôri d…ô n…ôz…ôr…ô alńĪr. Dijkstra m√ľsb…ôt √ß…ôkili qraflarda daha s√ľr…ôtlidir, Bellman-Ford is…ô m…ônfi √ß…ôkil…ôr v…ô d√∂vr aŇükarlamaq √ľ√ß√ľn uyńüundur.

QonŇüuluq matrisi il…ô vaxt m√ľr…ôkk…ôbliyi O(V¬≤)-dir, burada V n√∂qt…ôl…ôrin sayńĪdńĪr, √ß√ľnki h…ôr n√∂qt…ônin qonŇüularńĪ yoxlanńĪlńĪr. Prioritet n√∂vb…ôsi (heap) il…ô O((V + E) log V) olur, burada E k…ônarlarńĪn sayńĪdńĪr. M…ôs…ôl…ôn, 5 n√∂qt…ôli v…ô 7 k…ônarlńĪ qrafda Dijkstra O(25) v…ô ya heap il…ô O(12 log 5) …ôm…ôliyyat aparńĪr. Bellman-Ford-un vaxt m√ľr…ôkk…ôbliyi O(V * E)-dir, bu da b√∂y√ľk qraflarda yavaŇü ola bil…ôr. M…ôkan m√ľr…ôkk…ôbliyi h…ôr iki alqoritmd…ô O(V)-dir (m…ôsaf…ô v…ô visited massivl…ôri √ľ√ß√ľn). Backtracking il…ô m√ľqayis…ôd…ô (m…ôs…ôl…ôn, O(N!) N-Queens √ľ√ß√ľn), …ôn qńĪsa yol alqoritml…ôri daha s…ôm…ôr…ôlidir. Dinamik proqramlaŇüdńĪrma il…ô m√ľqayis…ôd…ô (m…ôs…ôl…ôn, O(n * m) Minimum sikk…ô problemind…ô) Dijkstra daha az yaddaŇü t…ôl…ôb edir, lakin Bellman-Ford daha √ßox hesablama t…ôl…ôb ed…ô bil…ôr.
∆Źn qńĪsa yol alqoritml…ôrinin √ľst√ľnl√ľkl…ôri v…ô m…ônfi c…ôh…ôtl…ôri

en.title: Shortest Path Algorithms
en.description: Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, and other shortest path algorithms find the shortest path in weighted graphs, exemplified by Dijkstra for shortest path finding.

codes.php:
<?php

function dijkstra($graph, $start, $target) {
  $vertices = count($graph);
  $dist = array_fill(0, $vertices, PHP_INT_MAX);
  $visited = array_fill(0, $vertices, false);
  $dist[$start] = 0;
  for ($count = 0; $count < $vertices - 1; $count++) {
    $min = PHP_INT_MAX;
    $u = 0;
    for ($i = 0; $i < $vertices; $i++) {
      if (!$visited[$i] && $dist[$i] <= $min) {
        $min = $dist[$i];
        $u = $i;
      }
    }
    $visited[$u] = true;
    for ($v = 0; $v < $vertices; $v++) {
      if (!$visited[$v] && $graph[$u][$v] && $dist[$u] != PHP_INT_MAX && $dist[$u] + $graph[$u][$v] < $dist[$v]) {
        $dist[$v] = $dist[$u] + $graph[$u][$v];
      }
    }
  }
  if ($dist[$target] == PHP_INT_MAX) {
    echo "Target not reachable\n";
  } else {
    echo "Distance to target: " . $dist[$target] . "\n";
  }
}

$graph = [
  [0, 4, 2, 0, 0],
  [4, 0, 5, 10, 0],
  [2, 5, 0, 3, 8],
  [0, 10, 3, 0, 6],
  [0, 0, 8, 6, 0]
];
dijkstra($graph, 0, 4);
// Output: Distance to target: 8

content.text: What are Shortest Path Algorithms?

Shortest path algorithms find the shortest path between two nodes in a weighted graph, where edges have weights (e.g., representing distances between cities). A graph consists of nodes (vertices) and weighted edges. The most well-known algorithms are Dijkstra and Bellman-Ford. Dijkstra‚Äôs algorithm finds the shortest path in graphs with non-negative weights using a greedy approach. Bellman-Ford handles negative weights and can detect negative cycles. For example, Dijkstra can find the shortest distance between two cities (e.g., from node 0 to node 4, distance 8). Compared to backtracking, shortest path algorithms are more efficient as they don‚Äôt explore all possible paths. Compared to dynamic programming, Dijkstra uses less memory, but Bellman-Ford may require more computation.
How Do Shortest Path Algorithms Work?

Dijkstra‚Äôs algorithm works as follows: 1) **Start node** - the search begins at the start node (e.g., node 0). 2) **Distance array** - an array stores the shortest distances to each node, with the start node‚Äôs distance set to 0 and others to infinity. 3) **Greedy selection** - at each step, the unvisited node with the smallest distance is selected. 4) **Distance update** - distances to the selected node‚Äôs neighbors are updated if a shorter path is found. For example, in a 5-node graph, Dijkstra finds the path from node 0 to node 4 (e.g., 0 ‚Üí 2 ‚Üí 4, distance 8). Bellman-Ford iteratively checks all edges and handles negative weights. Dijkstra is faster for non-negative weights, while Bellman-Ford is suitable for negative weights and cycle detection.
Efficiency of Shortest Path Algorithms (Big O)

The efficiency of Dijkstra‚Äôs algorithm depends on the graph representation. With an adjacency matrix, the time complexity is O(V¬≤), where V is the number of vertices, as all neighbors are checked. With a priority queue (heap), it‚Äôs O((V + E) log V), where E is the number of edges. For example, in a 5-node, 7-edge graph, Dijkstra takes O(25) or O(12 log 5) operations with a heap. Bellman-Ford‚Äôs time complexity is O(V * E), which can be slow for large graphs. Space complexity for both is O(V) for distance and visited arrays. Compared to backtracking (e.g., O(N!) for N-Queens), shortest path algorithms are more efficient. Compared to dynamic programming (e.g., O(n * m) for Minimum Coin Change), Dijkstra uses less memory, but Bellman-Ford may require more computation.
Advantages and Disadvantages of Shortest Path Algorithms

items.description: Efficiency: Dijkstra is fast for non-negative weighted graphs.
Versatility: Bellman-Ford supports negative weights and cycle detection.
Disadvantage: Dijkstra doesn‚Äôt work with negative weights.
Disadvantage: Bellman-Ford is slow for large graphs (O(V * E)).

content.text: Shortest Path Algorithms in Real Life

Shortest path algorithms are widely used in navigation systems (e.g., Google Maps finding the shortest route between two points), network routing (e.g., sending data packets via the shortest path in the internet), and logistics (e.g., optimizing delivery routes). Dijkstra‚Äôs algorithm is effective for non-negative weighted graphs, such as road distances between cities. Bellman-Ford is used in scenarios with negative weights, like currency exchange arbitrage. Compared to backtracking, shortest path algorithms are more efficient as they don‚Äôt explore all paths. Compared to dynamic programming, Dijkstra uses less memory, but Bellman-Ford may require more computation. These algorithms are critical in modern technologies like GPS and network security.
Dangers and Recommendations

The main danger of shortest path algorithms is the computational load for large graphs. Dijkstra with an adjacency matrix requires O(V¬≤) time, and Bellman-Ford‚Äôs O(V * E) can be slower. Memory usage can also be an issue for large graphs. Recommendations include using a priority queue for Dijkstra to achieve O((V + E) log V) efficiency. Use Bellman-Ford for graphs with negative weights. Choose the algorithm based on the problem, e.g., Dijkstra for non-negative weights, Bellman-Ford for negative weights or cycle detection. Test the algorithm with different graph sizes to evaluate performance. If speed is a priority, explore heuristic algorithms like A*, but verify they provide optimal solutions.

<?php

function dijkstra($graph, $start, $target) {
  $vertices = count($graph);
  $dist = array_fill(0, $vertices, PHP_INT_MAX);
  $visited = array_fill(0, $vertices, false);
  $dist[$start] = 0;
  for ($count = 0; $count < $vertices - 1; $count++) {
    $min = PHP_INT_MAX;
    $u = 0;
    for ($i = 0; $i < $vertices; $i++) {
      if (!$visited[$i] && $dist[$i] <= $min) {
        $min = $dist[$i];
        $u = $i;
      }
    }
    $visited[$u] = true;
    for ($v = 0; $v < $vertices; $v++) {
      if (!$visited[$v] && $graph[$u][$v] && $dist[$u] != PHP_INT_MAX && $dist[$u] + $graph[$u][$v] < $dist[$v]) {
        $dist[$v] = $dist[$u] + $graph[$u][$v];
      }
    }
  }
  if ($dist[$target] == PHP_INT_MAX) {
    echo "Target not reachable\n";
  } else {
    echo "Distance to target: " . $dist[$target] . "\n";
  }
}

$graph = [
  [0, 4, 2, 0, 0],
  [4, 0, 5, 10, 0],
  [2, 5, 0, 3, 8],
  [0, 10, 3, 0, 6],
  [0, 0, 8, 6, 0]

dijkstra($graph, 0, 4);
// Output: Distance to target: 8

–ź–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ Dijkstra —Ä–į–Ī–ĺ—ā–į–Ķ—ā —Ā–Ľ–Ķ–ī—É—é—Č–ł–ľ –ĺ–Ī—Ä–į–∑–ĺ–ľ: 1) **–Ě–į—á–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ–į—Ź –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ–į** ‚ÄĒ –Ņ–ĺ–ł—Ā–ļ –Ĺ–į—á–ł–Ĺ–į–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ź —Ā –Ĺ–į—á–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ—č (–Ĺ–į–Ņ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ—č 0). 2) **–ú–į—Ā—Ā–ł–≤ —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł–Ļ** ‚ÄĒ —Ā–ĺ–∑–ī–į—Ď—ā—Ā—Ź –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–ł–≤ –ī–Ľ—Ź —Ö—Ä–į–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł—Ź –ļ—Ä–į—ā—á–į–Ļ—ą–ł—Ö —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł–Ļ –ī–ĺ –ļ–į–∂–ī–ĺ–Ļ –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ—č, —Ā –Ĺ–į—á–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ —Ä–į–≤–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ 0 –ł –ĺ—Ā—ā–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ—č–ľ–ł —Ä–į–≤–Ĺ—č–ľ–ł –Ī–Ķ—Ā–ļ–ĺ–Ĺ–Ķ—á–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā–ł. 3) **–Ė–į–ī–Ĺ—č–Ļ –≤—č–Ī–ĺ—Ä** ‚ÄĒ –Ĺ–į –ļ–į–∂–ī–ĺ–ľ —ą–į–≥–Ķ –≤—č–Ī–ł—Ä–į–Ķ—ā—Ā—Ź –Ĺ–Ķ–Ņ–ĺ—Ā–Ķ—Č—Ď–Ĺ–Ĺ–į—Ź –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ–į —Ā –Ĺ–į–ł–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ć—ą–ł–ľ —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł–Ķ–ľ. 4) **–ě–Ī–Ĺ–ĺ–≤–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ķ —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł–Ļ** ‚ÄĒ —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł—Ź –ī–ĺ —Ā–ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ī–Ķ–Ļ –≤—č–Ī—Ä–į–Ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ—č –ĺ–Ī–Ĺ–ĺ–≤–Ľ—Ź—é—ā—Ā—Ź, –Ķ—Ā–Ľ–ł –Ĺ–į–Ļ–ī–Ķ–Ĺ –Ī–ĺ–Ľ–Ķ–Ķ –ļ–ĺ—Ä–ĺ—ā–ļ–ł–Ļ –Ņ—É—ā—Ć. –Ě–į–Ņ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, –≤ –≥—Ä–į—Ą–Ķ —Ā 5 –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł Dijkstra –Ĺ–į—Ö–ĺ–ī–ł—ā –Ņ—É—ā—Ć –ĺ—ā –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ—č 0 –ī–ĺ –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ—č 4 (–Ĺ–į–Ņ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, 0 ‚Üí 2 ‚Üí 4, —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł–Ķ 8). –≠—Ą—Ą–Ķ–ļ—ā–ł–≤–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć –į–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ–į Dijkstra –∑–į–≤–ł—Ā–ł—ā –ĺ—ā –Ņ—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ā—ā–į–≤–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł—Ź –≥—Ä–į—Ą–į. –° –ľ–į—ā—Ä–ł—Ü–Ķ–Ļ —Ā–ľ–Ķ–∂–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā–ł –≤—Ä–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–į—Ź —Ā–Ľ–ĺ–∂–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć —Ā–ĺ—Ā—ā–į–≤–Ľ—Ź–Ķ—ā O(V¬≤), –≥–ī–Ķ V ‚ÄĒ –ļ–ĺ–Ľ–ł—á–Ķ—Ā—ā–≤–ĺ –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ, —ā–į–ļ –ļ–į–ļ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≤–Ķ—Ä—Ź—é—ā—Ā—Ź –≤—Ā–Ķ —Ā–ĺ—Ā–Ķ–ī–ł. –° –Ņ—Ä–ł–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–Ķ—ā–Ĺ–ĺ–Ļ –ĺ—á–Ķ—Ä–Ķ–ī—Ć—é (heap) ‚ÄĒ O((V + E) log V), –≥–ī–Ķ E ‚ÄĒ –ļ–ĺ–Ľ–ł—á–Ķ—Ā—ā–≤–ĺ —Ä—Ď–Ī–Ķ—Ä. –Ě–į–Ņ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, –≤ –≥—Ä–į—Ą–Ķ —Ā 5 –≤–Ķ—Ä—ą–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł –ł 7 —Ä—Ď–Ī—Ä–į–ľ–ł Dijkstra –∑–į–Ĺ–ł–ľ–į–Ķ—ā O(25) –ł–Ľ–ł O(12 log 5) –ĺ–Ņ–Ķ—Ä–į—Ü–ł–Ļ —Ā heap. –í—Ä–Ķ–ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–į—Ź —Ā–Ľ–ĺ–∂–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć Bellman-Ford ‚ÄĒ O(V * E), —á—ā–ĺ –ľ–ĺ–∂–Ķ—ā –Ī—č—ā—Ć –ľ–Ķ–ī–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–ĺ –ī–Ľ—Ź –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ć—ą–ł—Ö –≥—Ä–į—Ą–ĺ–≤. –ü—Ä–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ä–į–Ĺ—Ā—ā–≤–Ķ–Ĺ–Ĺ–į—Ź —Ā–Ľ–ĺ–∂–Ĺ–ĺ—Ā—ā—Ć –ī–Ľ—Ź –ĺ–Ī–ĺ–ł—Ö ‚ÄĒ O(V) –ī–Ľ—Ź –ľ–į—Ā—Ā–ł–≤–ĺ–≤ —Ä–į—Ā—Ā—ā–ĺ—Ź–Ĺ–ł–Ļ –ł –Ņ–ĺ—Ā–Ķ—Č–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ļ. –ü–ĺ —Ā—Ä–į–≤–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł—é —Ā –Ī—ć–ļ—ā—Ä–Ķ–ļ–ł–Ĺ–≥–ĺ–ľ (–Ĺ–į–Ņ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, O(N!) –ī–Ľ—Ź –∑–į–ī–į—á–ł N —Ą–Ķ—Ä–∑–Ķ–Ļ), –į–Ľ–≥–ĺ—Ä–ł—ā–ľ—č –ļ—Ä–į—ā—á–į–Ļ—ą–Ķ–≥–ĺ –Ņ—É—ā–ł —ć—Ą—Ą–Ķ–ļ—ā–ł–≤–Ĺ–Ķ–Ķ. –ü–ĺ —Ā—Ä–į–≤–Ĺ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł—é —Ā –ī–ł–Ĺ–į–ľ–ł—á–Ķ—Ā–ļ–ł–ľ –Ņ—Ä–ĺ–≥—Ä–į–ľ–ľ–ł—Ä–ĺ–≤–į–Ĺ–ł–Ķ–ľ (–Ĺ–į–Ņ—Ä–ł–ľ–Ķ—Ä, O(n * m) –ī–Ľ—Ź –∑–į–ī–į—á–ł –ĺ –ľ–ł–Ĺ–ł–ľ–į–Ľ—Ć–Ĺ–ĺ–ľ –ļ–ĺ–Ľ–ł—á–Ķ—Ā—ā–≤–Ķ –ľ–ĺ–Ĺ–Ķ—ā), Dijkstra –ł—Ā–Ņ–ĺ–Ľ—Ć–∑—É–Ķ—ā –ľ–Ķ–Ĺ—Ć—ą–Ķ –Ņ–į–ľ—Ź—ā–ł, –Ĺ–ĺ Bellman-Ford –ľ–ĺ–∂–Ķ—ā —ā—Ä–Ķ–Ī–ĺ–≤–į—ā—Ć –Ī–ĺ–Ľ—Ć—ą–Ķ –≤—č—á–ł—Ā–Ľ–Ķ–Ĺ–ł–Ļ.
